sections.name: Header
sections.description: Sticky header with customer name, report date, and bureau toggle
sections.style: bg-white border-b border-slate-200 sticky top-0 z-10

sections.name: RiskSummaryHero
sections.description: Top section containing Credit Score Gauge, Risk Band, and Critical Alerts
sections.layout: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-4 p-6 bg-slate-50/50

elements.name: ScoreGauge
elements.details: Semi-circle gauge with score value in center. Color coded based on risk.

elements.name: KeyMetrics
elements.details: 4-column grid showing Total Accounts, Active Loans, Total Overdue, Recent Inquiries

sections.name: RedFlagsSection
sections.description: Dedicated section for negative indicators. ONLY visible if issues exist.
sections.style: bg-red-50 border border-red-100 rounded-xl p-4 mx-6 mb-6

PaymentHistoryHeatmap.description: Horizontal strip of colored blocks representing last 24-36 months

RiskBandBadge.description: Pill-shaped badge with risk level text

instructions_to_main_agent: Refactor `CreditScoreModal.jsx` to use the new design system.
Maintain all existing logic for OTP, API calls, and data parsing. DO NOT BREAK the `DualBureauReportView` logic.
Replace the existing `ScoreCard` with a more professional `RiskSummaryHero`.
Implement a `RedFlagsSection` that dynamically appears when `problemAccounts.length > 0` or `totalOverdue > 0`.
Redesign the Accounts List to be a dense table/grid with 'Payment History' visualized as a mini-heatmap inline.
Use `lucide-react` icons for all visual indicators.
Ensure all text colors meet APCA contrast guidelines (no grey-on-grey text).
Add `data-testid` to all interactive elements (tabs, buttons, inputs, expanded rows).
Use `JetBrains Mono` for all financial figures (amounts, dates, account numbers).
Do not use `transparent` backgrounds for dropdowns or modals.
Ensure the modal is responsive (stack columns on mobile, grid on desktop).
universal_guidelines: Avoid 'AI Slop' - no generic centered layouts.
Use 2-3x more spacing (padding) inside containers than standard.
Use specific transitions (e.g., `transition-colors duration-200`) instead of `transition-all`.
Never use pure black (#000000). Use #0F172A or #1E293B.
Interactive elements must have clear hover and focus states.
